message:
fix(doctor): add post-repair write probe and extend corruption detection (#245)

Addresses issue #245 where REINDEX could fix index ordering enough for
PRAGMA integrity_check to pass, but the underlying B-tree corruption
caused writes to silently fail (reads worked, writes got ISSUE_NOT_FOUND).

doctor.rs:
- Broaden report_has_page_corruption() to detect "malformed", "out of
  order", and "disk image" in addition to "free space corruption" — these
  are all symptoms of frankensqlite B-tree divergences from C sqlite3
- Add write_probe_after_repair(): after VACUUM/REINDEX, insert a probe
  row into the issues table, read it back, and delete it inside a single
  BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction. If any step fails, the repair is deemed
  insufficient and the doctor falls through to a full JSONL rebuild
  instead of declaring success
- Update comments to reference both #237 and #245

sync.rs:
- Extend the table-reset path (drop+recreate data tables before import)
  to trigger on --rebuild in addition to --force. Without this, --rebuild
  on a corrupt DB could hang indefinitely during orphan deletion because
  frankensqlite's DELETE cursor hits the same B-tree corruption that
  causes write failures
- Preserve tombstones across rebuild imports the same way force does

storage/sqlite.rs:
- Add diagnostic tracing::warn in update_issue() when get_issue_from_conn
  returns None inside a write transaction — this is the specific symptom
  of #245 (read path succeeds, write-transaction read fails) and now
  suggests `br doctor --repair` in the log message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
